title: [Rampardos] Battle Guide
summary: A one-shot physical [Rock] wallbreaker built on base 165 Atk and [sheer-force] — [head-smash] loses its re[coil] and hits like a finisher on contact.
strengths: Base 165 Atk is the top of the physical chart in the format
[sheer-force] nullifies [head-smash] re[coil] and boosts [zen-headbutt], [stone-edge], and [crunch] by 1.3x
Cove[rage] options line up cleanly: [earthquake], [zen-headbutt], and [crunch] cover every common [Rock] resist

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Rampardos] exists for exactly one reason: base 165 Atk is the highest physical Attack stat in the format, and [sheer-force] turns [head-smash] into a re[coil]-free STAB nuke. Base 58 Speed and 60 Defense are frankly terrible, so every game with [Rampardos] is about landing exactly one [head-smash] at the right moment and accepting that the trade is often the first plan. You run [Rampardos] as a dedicated [sing]le-target wallbreaker, not as a sweeper.

## Strengths
Stacked with Jolly and STAB, [sheer-force] [head-smash] is one of the highest [sing]le-hit damage numbers available, and because [sheer-force] cancels re[coil], [Rampardos] can actually double-tap when the situation allows. [earthquake] lets it punch through the [Steel], [Electric], and [Rock]-resist cores that normally wall mono-[Rock]. [zen-headbutt] and [crunch] both get the [sheer-force] multiplier too, which means [Rampardos] does not lose damage when it has to pivot into a cove[rage] slot instead of the main STAB.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Everything stems from the defensive spread. 97/60/50 bulk means any 2x hit from [Fighting], [Ground], [Water], or [Grass] is a likely OHKO, and 58 Speed hands initiative to almost every offensive Pokemon in the tier. Priority moves like [mach-punch] or [bullet-punch] end [Rampardos] outright if [focus-sash] is already broken, and Scarf [Fighting] users shut the answer window completely.

## Recommended Moveset
[head-smash] is mandatory — everything else in the set is built a[round] the fact that [sheer-force] erases its re[coil] clause. [earthquake] is the second locked slot, handling the [Steel] and [Poison] walls that would otherwise sit on [Rock] STAB indefinitely. [zen-headbutt] is the [Fighting] answer and still benefits from [sheer-force], so it is not just filler cove[rage]. The final slot is [crunch] to punch through [Psychic]s and [Ghost]s; the alternative is [stone-edge] for a higher-damage secondary [Rock] attack when cove[rage] is less important than nuking.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the only correct item. 97/60 defenses make any other holdable effectively a one-turn cushion and sash at least guarantees [head-smash] one full send-off. [sheer-force] is locked and [mold-breaker] would cost too much damage to be worth considering. For Tera, [Rock] is the standard for the extra STAB multiplier on [head-smash], and [Ground] is the defensive swap that removes the [Fighting] weakness while still buffing [earthquake].

## Teammates & Synergy
[Rampardos] needs a guaranteed entry turn, so [fake-out] users and slow pivots are mandatory support — anything that removes an oppo[sing] priority threat in advance. A setup sweeper in the back of the team should be ready to capitalize on the pace [head-smash] creates, since [Rampardos] itself cannot clean. A [Ground]-resistant pivot [round]s out the team by [soak]ing the attacks [Rampardos] cannot switch into.

## Play Patterns
Do not send [Rampardos] in while oppo[sing] [Fighting] or [Ground] threats are healthy — wait until the [counter] list is gone, then drop it in on a predicted wall and fire [head-smash] for a forced KO. With [focus-sash] intact you can get a second turn — usually [earthquake] or [zen-headbutt] — but plan every game a[round] the assumption that [Rampardos] only gets one real action before exiting.
